Overall, wi_skills produces slightly more code-anchored and precise output, making it marginally more effective, though the gap between the two is smaller than anticipated.

utility_details.judge_error: Both documents are truncated in the display, so J3.2 and J3.6 for wi_skills are partially inferred from the file size (10,143 bytes vs 8,552 bytes), trajectory summary, and visible content. The wi_skills file is notably larger and the agent summary references stripping and lumber spec sections. The wo_skills run failed verifier check V3.4 (no code/standard reference), consistent with the absence of OSHA/IBC/ACI citations in the visible content.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is substantially more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run produced a larger, more technically detailed document (10,143 bytes vs 8,552 bytes) that passed all 5 verifier checks (wo_skills failed V3.4). On the judge evaluation items, wi_skills scores 5/7 while wo_skills scores only 1/7. Key differentiators: wi_skills explicitly differentiates jack stud requirements by opening size, specifies lumber species/grade/moisture content, references OSHA fall protection at specific elevations, includes form stripping criteria with time/strength thresholds, and provides header type recommendations with load rationale. Both documents fail J3.1 (no calculated hydrostatic pressure-based tie spacing), and both pass J3.5 (trade handoff deliverables). The skill provided professional-depth knowledge that produced an actionable brief rather than a high-level overview, consistent with the expected outcome described in the test rationale.
